ocr.text: In the Rare disease franchise, RareDrugA generated revenue of $15M with a net income of $5M, achieving a market share of
20% and a growth rate of 10%, alongside R&D spend of $2M, sales volume of 1,500 units, and an average price of $10,000,
launched in Australia in 2018. RareDrugB reported revenue of $10M, net income of $3M, market share of 15%, growth rate of
8%, R&D spend of $1M, sales volume of 800 units, and an average price of $12,500, launched in New Zealand in 2017.

RareDrugA  15M  5M  20%  10%  2M  1,500  10,000  Australia  2018
RareDrugB  10M  3M  15%  8%  1M  800  12,500  NewZealand  2017
RareDrugC  20M  7M  25%  12%  3M  1,200  15,000  Japan  2019
RareDrugD  12M  4M  18%  9%  1.5M  950  11,000  SouthKorea  2020
RareDrugE  5M  1M  10%  5%  0.5M  600  8,000  Singapore  2021
RareDrugF  8M  2M  12%  6%  0.8M  700  9,500  HongKong  2022
RareDrugC, launched in Japan in 2019, achieved revenue of $20M, net income of $7M, a market share of 25%, growth rate of
12%, R&D spend of $3M, sales volume of 1,200 units, and an average price of $15,000. RareDrugD, launched in South Korea
in 2020, attained revenue of $12M, net income of $4M, market share of 18%, growth rate of 9%, R&D spend of $1.5M, sales
volume of 950 units, and an average price of $11,000. RareDrugE reported $5M in revenue, $1M in net income, a market share
of 10%, growth rate of 5%, R&D spend of $0.5M, sales volume of 600 units, and an average price of $8,000, launched in
Singapore in 2021. RareDrugF generated $8M in revenue, $2M in net income, a market share of 12%, growth rate of 6%, R&D
spend of $0.8M, sales volume of 700 units, and an average price of $9,500, launched in Hong Kong in 2022. In the Oncology
portfolio, Keytruda achieved $600M in sales with a market share of 15.0%, a growth rate of 40.0%, and regional performance at
30.0% in Australia. Atezolizumab reported $200M, with market share of 5.0%, growth rate of 15.0%, and regional performance
at 12.0% in New Zealand.

Year-EndSummary  $1.5B  20.0  10.0  18.0  SignificantGrowth  OverallPerformance
Nivolumab generated $150M with a market share of 10.0%, growth rate of 8.0%, and regional performance at 7.0% in
Singapore. Durvalumab achieved $80M, with a market share of 2.0%, growth rate of 5.0%, and regional performance at 4.0%
in Japan. Trastuzumab reported $100M, with a market share of 3.0%, growth rate of 6.0%, and regional performance at 5.0% in
South Korea. The total oncology portfolio reached $1.23B, with a market share of 35.0%, growth rate of 15.0%, and regional
performance at 25.0% in Asia-Pacific. The Year-End Summary indicated a total of $1.5B with a 20.0% market share, 10.0%
growth, and significant growth noted in overall performance.
